findings.description: Repeated header/footer content from PDF page breaks rendered as inline body content between the logo and the MEMORANDUM heading, disrupting reading order. The organizational header information (PAT McCRORY/Governor, DONALD R. VAN DER VAART/Secretary, TRACY DAVIS/Director) appears twice — once at the top of the HTML and again after inserted footer text and a decorative banner — before the MEMORANDUM heading.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the letterhead header, then MEMORANDUM, then memo content, with footer only at the page bottom. The HTML inserts page-1 footer text ('State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality…') and a decorative gradient banner, followed by a second rendering of the organizational header, between the logo and the MEMORANDUM heading.

findings.description: Footer text and decorative banner from a PDF page break are rendered as body content between the 'I. History/Background' section heading and the beginning of its body text, creating a reading-order disruption with a large blank gap and extraneous footer information splitting the section.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows 'I. History/Background' immediately followed by the paragraph beginning 'Under the historic Coal Ash Management Act (CAMA) of 2014…'. In the HTML, a large blank space, footer text ('State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality…'), and a decorative colored banner are inserted between the heading and this paragraph.

findings.description: Spurious image inserted at the start of Section I (History/Background) that has no corresponding substantive image in the PDF. The PDF's Section I begins directly with text; no image or figure appears there.
findings.evidence: HTML contains at the beginning of the History/Background section. The PDF page 2 has no image at the start of Section I — only the heading and body text.
